_description: Common task patterns and workflow templates for AI-Hydro

delineate_single_watershed.description: Delineate watershed boundary for a single gauge
steps: Validate: Check area_km2 matches expected drainage area
Save: Optionally save as shapefile with save_shapefile=True

extract_single_attribute_type.description: Extract one type of attribute (e.g., topography)
steps: First delineate watershed to get boundary geometry
Validate results are within expected ranges
extract_single_attribute_type.example:
from ai_hydro.tools.topography import extract_topographic_attributes
attrs, units, dem, slope = extract_topographic_attributes(watershed_geometry)

fetch_time_series.description: Fetch streamflow or climate time series
fetch_time_series.example:
from ai_hydro.tools.hydrology import fetch_streamflow_data
q = fetch_streamflow_data('01031500', '2010-01-01', '2020-12-31')

camels_attribute_extraction.description: Extract all CAMELS attributes for a gauge

steps: Extract all static attributes (topo, soil, geology, veg)

regional_analysis.description: Analyze multiple basins in a region
considerations: Implement error handling for each basin
Store partial results to allow resume

sensitivity_analysis.description: Test sensitivity to parameters or data sources
steps: Run workflow with different parameter sets

data_quality_assessment.description: Comprehensive quality check of watershed data
steps: Apply validation checks to each attribute

approach: Use watershed boundary to clip external data

frameworks.SUMMA: Extract meteorological forcing and basin attributes
frameworks.VIC: Prepare soil parameters and forcing data
frameworks.HBV: Extract precipitation, temperature, and basin characteristics
frameworks.GR4J: Prepare daily P, E, Q data

step_by_step_validation.description: Validate each step of a workflow

data_inspection.description: Inspect data quality at each stage
checks: Check for NaN or invalid values

parallel_processing.example:
from concurrent.futures import ProcessPoolExecutor
with ProcessPoolExecutor(max_workers=4) as executor:
    results = executor.map(process_gauge, gauge_list)

strategies: Delete variables when no longer needed
Use appropriate data types (int8 vs int64)
Stream data instead of loading all at once

approach: Create test fixtures with known data
unit_testing.example:
def test_delineate_watershed():
    result = delineate_watershed('01031500')
    assert 'area_km2' in result
    assert result['area_km2'] > 0
